title: Sequences & Series of Functions
description: Module 3 — theorem spine plus canonical examples and counterexamples

nodes.label:
Absolute and Uniform
on Compact Subintervals

edges.label: if you fix \(x\) first and then let \(n\to\infty\), what type of convergence is this?
edges.hint: The index threshold may depend on the point \(x\).

edges.label: if one \(N\) works for every \(x\in E\), what type of convergence is this?
edges.hint: The same index bound works across the whole domain.

edges.label: in the equivalent criterion, \(\sup_{x\in E}|f_n(x)-f(x)|\) tends to what value?
edges.hint: Uniform convergence is convergence in the sup norm.

edges.label: the function \(f(x)=\lim_n f_n(x)\) is called the ___ function; fill in the missing word.
edges.hint: It is defined point by point from the sequence.

edges.label: a uniform limit of continuous functions is automatically what?
edges.hint: This is the standard uniform limit theorem.

edges.label: on a compact interval, uniform convergence lets the limit and integral do what?
edges.hint: You can swap the order of the two operations.

edges.label: to conclude \(f_n'\to f'\), uniform convergence of \(f_n\) alone is what?
edges.hint: You also need control of the derivative sequence, plus convergence at a point.

edges.label: a sum \(\sum f_n(x)\) is called a ___ of functions; fill in the term.
edges.hint: Its convergence is defined through partial sums.

edges.label: the sequence used to test convergence is the sequence of ___ sums; fill in the term.
edges.hint: Stop the infinite sum at a finite index \(N\).

edges.label: if \(|f_n(x)|\le M_n\) and \(\sum M_n\) converges, then \(\sum f_n\) converges ___ and absolutely.
edges.hint: This is exactly what the Weierstrass M-test gives you.

edges.label: a power series has terms of the form coefficient times what?
edges.hint: The exponent is the varying part; the center stays fixed.

edges.label: in \(\sum a_n(x-c)^n\), the constant \(c\) is called the ___ of the series.
edges.hint: The powers are measured relative to this point.

edges.label: when \(|x-c|\) is less than which constant?
edges.hint: This number sets the interior convergence region.

edges.label: at the endpoints \(|x-c|=R\), convergence must be checked how?
edges.hint: The radius alone does not decide boundary behavior.

edges.label: on every closed interval strictly inside \(|x-c|<R\), convergence is absolute and what kind of convergence?
edges.hint: This is the local uniformity that makes power series well behaved.

edges.label: inside the interval of convergence, power series can be differentiated and integrated ___ by term.
edges.hint: The operation is applied one summand at a time.

edges.label: if \(a_n=f^{(n)}(c)/n!\), the power series is the ___ series of \(f\).
edges.hint: Its coefficients come from derivatives at the center.

edges.label: the finite truncation of a Taylor series is called the Taylor ___.
edges.hint: It is the partial sum through degree \(n\).

edges.label: a Taylor series centered at \(0\) is called a ___ series.
edges.hint: It is the special case centered at the origin.

edges.label: the approximation error \(f(x)-T_n(x)\) is called the ___ term.
edges.hint: It measures what the polynomial approximation leaves out.

edges.label: Taylor's theorem gives a formula or bound for the ___ term.
edges.hint: This is the quantitative control of approximation error.

edges.label: if a function equals its Taylor series on a neighborhood, it is ___.
edges.hint: Smoothness alone is not enough; equality with the series is the key.

edges.label: classic example separating modes: \(f_n(x)=x^n\) on \([0,1]\) converges in what sense?
edges.hint: The threshold depends on how close \(x\) is to 1.

edges.label: for this example, the limit value on \([0,1)\) is ___.
edges.hint: This jump at \(x=1\) explains why convergence is not uniform on \([0,1]\).

edges.label: on \(|x|\le R\), choose \(M_n=R^n/n!\); then the \(e^x\) series converges ___.
edges.hint: The majorant \(\sum R^n/n!\) converges.

edges.label: for \(\ln(1+x)\) around 0, what is the radius of convergence?
edges.hint: Converges for \(|x|<1\), with endpoint behavior checked separately.

edges.label: all derivatives at 0 can vanish while \(f\neq\sum T_n\); smooth does NOT imply ___.
edges.hint: This is the standard real-analysis counterexample.
